verses.text: Wars come from 'lusts that war in your members'; you have not because you ask not, or ask amiss 'to consume it upon your lusts.' Wrong wanting. Selfish prayer goes unanswered.
Wars come from 'lusts that war in your members'; you have not because you ask not, or ask amiss 'to consume it upon your lusts.' Wrong wanting. Selfish prayer goes unanswered.
Wars come from 'lusts that war in your members'; you have not because you ask not, or ask amiss 'to consume it upon your lusts.' Wrong wanting. Selfish prayer goes unanswered.
'Friendship of the world is enmity with God'; to love the world is to be His enemy. Choose. The heart cannot belong to both God and the world.
'Friendship of the world is enmity with God'; to love the world is to be His enemy. Choose. The heart cannot belong to both God and the world.
'God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.' 'Submit yourselves... to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee.' Humble grace. The lowly receive grace and victory.
'God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.' 'Submit yourselves... to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee.' Humble grace. The lowly receive grace and victory.
'Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you'; cleanse your hands, 'humble yourselves... and he shall lift you up.' Come near. God exalts the contrite who seek Him.
'Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you'; cleanse your hands, 'humble yourselves... and he shall lift you up.' Come near. God exalts the contrite who seek Him.
'Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you'; cleanse your hands, 'humble yourselves... and he shall lift you up.' Come near. God exalts the contrite who seek Him.
Speak not evil of one another; 'there is one lawgiver,' able to save and destroy. Who are you to judge? One Judge. Slander usurps the seat of God.
Speak not evil of one another; 'there is one lawgiver,' able to save and destroy. Who are you to judge? One Judge. Slander usurps the seat of God.
Boast not of tomorrow; 'what is your life? It is even a vapour.' Say rather, 'If the Lord will, we shall... do this.' Fleeting life. Plans must bow to God's will.
Boast not of tomorrow; 'what is your life? It is even a vapour.' Say rather, 'If the Lord will, we shall... do this.' Fleeting life. Plans must bow to God's will.
Boast not of tomorrow; 'what is your life? It is even a vapour.' Say rather, 'If the Lord will, we shall... do this.' Fleeting life. Plans must bow to God's will.
Now you rejoice in your boastings; 'to him that knoweth to do good, and doeth it not, to him it is sin.' Known duty. Neglected good is itself a sin.
